Your Transit Custom. Your kitchen. Ready when you are.

Our Rear Pull-Out Kitchen Pod is designed for Transit Custom owners who want a proper cooking setup without sacrificing the full load area.

Slide it out when you arrive. Slide it away when you leave.

What you get:
✔ 300+ litres of storage
✔ Built for a twin burner hob (you supply the hob)
✔ Laser-cut aluminium construction
✔ Heavy-duty runners rated to 220kg
✔ Textured black powder-coat finish

At £649, it's one of the most practical upgrades you can make to a Transit Custom conversion — especially if the van does double duty as a work vehicle during the week.

Explore it at roamsystems.co.uk/products/rear-pull-out-kitchen-pod

socialPosts.caption:
Quick question: is the rear seating in your campervan actually legal to carry passengers?

If it isn't M1 certified to ECE R14.09 standard, the honest answer is probably no.

M1 certification is the same safety testing that manufacturers apply to seats fitted in new vehicles. It covers crash loading, belt anchorage strength, and structural integrity — not just whether the seat feels solid.

For Transit Custom conversions, this matters because:

→ Uncertified rear seating may void your insurance if passengers are injured
→ DVSA inspectors can flag non-compliant seating at roadside checks
→ If you're converting for family use with child seats, ISOFIX points need to be load-tested too

Our M1 Certified U-Shape Seating Frame is the only M1 certified U-shape frame on the UK market. It fits the Ford Transit Custom as well as VW T5, T6, and T6.1 — with integrated seatbelts and ISOFIX points built in.

More on M1 certification: roamsystems.co.uk/products/m1-certified-u-shape-seating-frame
socialPosts.imageSuggestion: Close-up of the ISOFIX anchorage points and 3-point seatbelt integrated into the U-shape frame. Clean product shot on white or inside a Transit Custom.

socialPosts.caption:
There's something about a Transit Custom that just makes sense.

Small enough to park almost anywhere. Big enough for a proper bed, a kitchen, and everything you need for the weekend.

The Ford Transit Custom has become one of the most popular conversion bases in the UK — and it's not hard to see why. You can drive it to work Monday morning and have it ready for the Peak District by Friday afternoon.

Where are you taking yours this spring?

Whether you're mid-build or just starting to plan, we've put together a full furniture guide for Transit Custom conversions over on the blog — covering seating, kitchen options, and storage setups that actually work in the space.

Link in bio. Have a great weekend. 🚐
